generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing spatial perception for real-time environmental mapping via 2D/3D point cloud data generation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance DepthCameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i depth camera providing depth perception for real-time environmental mapping"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state sensors (speed, position, orientation) providing real-time kinematic data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor and servo actuation feedback sensors providing torque, angle, and response validation data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationFeedback
]

instance BatteryStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Battery management system sensors providing state-of-charge (SoC) and health metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData BatteryStateData
]

instance ManufacturingEquipmentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial equipment health sensors providing operational metrics for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentHealthData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted via Wi-Fi (UDP) at ≥10Hz frequency for real-time processing"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw depth camera data transmitted via Wi-Fi (UDP) for environmental mapping"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DepthCameraSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data (speed, position, orientation) transmitted via ROS2 topics at ≥10Hz"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationFeedback : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor and servo actuation feedback data transmitted via ROS2 topics for closed-loop validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance BatteryStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Battery state data (SoC, voltage, temperature) transmitted via ROS2 topics for energy management"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BatteryStateSensor
]

instance EquipmentHealthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Manufacturing equipment health metrics transmitted via ROS2 topics for predictive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingEquipmentSensor
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks generated from LiDAR/DepthCamera data via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor, DepthCameraSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model for LiDAR point cloud clustering and object tracking implemented in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter for state estimation (vehicle trajectory, battery SoC) with ROS2 integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance BatteryDegradationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based or data-driven model for battery degradation forecasting"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryManagementEnabler
]

instance FailurePredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital twin model trained on historical data for industrial equipment failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveMaintenanceEnabler
]

instance TrajectoryPlanningModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Collision avoidance trajectory planning model for aerospace and autonomous vehicle applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Pre-processed LiDAR point cloud data after noise filtering and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler, TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler
]

instance DepthCameraDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Pre-processed depth camera data after noise reduction and spatial alignment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and validated vehicle state data for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateEstimationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and projected driving paths from point cloud processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance BatteryStateDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed battery state data for SoC balancing and thermal management"
    DTDFVocab:fromData BatteryStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatteryManagementEnabler
]

instance EquipmentHealthDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed equipment health metrics for predictive maintenance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EquipmentHealthData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveMaintenanceEnabler
]

instance HistoricalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical operational data stored in SQL databases for model training and validation"
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 framework enabling intra-entity communication between software components with 100ms synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AdaptiveControlService, TrajectoryOptimizationService
]

instance EdgeComputingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson-based edge computing platform for real-time sensor data processing"

]

instance WiFiCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance SimulinkProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink environment for point cloud clustering, tracking, and battery system modeling"

]

    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectDetectionService, TrajectoryOptimizationService
]

]

]

]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Trajectory planning algorithms for collision avoidance in aerospace and autonomous vehicles"
    DTDFVocab:enables CollisionAvoidanceService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization framework for digital replicas and simulation dashboards"

]

instance AIMLTrainingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TensorFlow/PyTorch-based AI/ML libraries for data-driven digital twin models"

]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) is governed by control flows originating from the Digital Twin Entity and targeting the Physical Twin Entity, primarily through the following mechanisms:

1. **Command Types and Control Flows**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the *Actuation FE* to control physical components such as motors, steering servos, and adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems. These commands are derived from processed data (e.g., object tracks, projected paths) and user inputs.
   - **Controlling FE Commands**: The *Controlling FE* (e.g., ACC logic) issues control signals to adjust vehicle behavior (e.g., acceleration/deceleration, steering corrections) based on real-time Digital Twin analysis.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are formatted and translated via the *Data Translation* component to ensure compatibility with physical actuators. Specific formats (e.g., ROS2 message types, UDP packets) are not detailed but are implied to align with the system’s communication infrastructure.

3. **Validation and Authentication**:
   - The document does not specify explicit validation or authentication mechanisms for control commands. However, the *Controlling FE* and *Actuation FE* imply a structured hierarchy where commands are vetted before execution (e.g., safety thresholds may be inherent in ACC logic).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - Physical actuators (e.g., motors, servos) provide feedback to the Digital Twin via the *Actuation FE*, closing the control loop. This feedback includes confirmation of command execution (e.g., achieved steering angle, motor RPM) and real-time state updates.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Not explicitly described in the document. Potential conflicts (e.g., competing commands from user and ACC) may be resolved via priority rules embedded in the *Controlling FE* or *Actuation FE*, though specifics are absent.

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - The *Actuation FE* likely generates acknowledgments or status updates upon successful command execution, which are propagated back to the Digital Twin for monitoring and logging.

**Key Functional Elements Involved**:
- *Controlling FE* (e.g., ACC logic, user input processing)
- *Actuation FE* (motor/servo control, command execution)
- *Data Translation* (command formatting and translation)
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards but not implemented here. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services, but the **ROS2-based architecture** (with individual ROS2 packages per Functional Entity, FE) implies real-time or near-real-time processing constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication within the **Physical Twin Entity**. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **100 ms synchronization window**, ensuring deterministic behavior for time-sensitive operations like actuation and point cloud clustering/tracking. No explicit mention of asynchronous or event-driven temporal patterns is provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its components using a **hybrid architecture** rooted in the **ISO 23247 RA**, where **Functional Entities (FEs)** are mapped to hardware components and implemented as **ROS2 packages**. Key deviations include the **Physical Twin Entity’s expanded role** (beyond OME’s resource-specific scope) and the **splitting of the User Interface FE** into visualization and control subcomponents. Inter-entity communication occurs over a **Wi-Fi network**, with no explicit mention of middleware-specific coordination (e.g., ROS2 topics/services or QoS policies). Resource management is implicit via the **entity-based decomposition**, where each hardware component hosts a dedicated entity, but no centralized orchestrator or dynamic resource allocation mechanisms are described. The system prioritizes **deterministic latency** (≤100 ms) over adaptive orchestration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The DT system must adhere to **real-time and high-fidelity** requirements, as digital twins are defined as computational reflections of physical assets with minimal latency. The **ISO 23247 framework** emphasizes modularity, suggesting **validation and verification** can be performed at the **Functional Entity (FE)** level. However, the document specifies **quantitative fidelity constraints** for the case study, including:
  - **Network latency**: ≤100 ms from the vehicle to the DT Entity (Requirement R7).
  - **Clock synchronization**: Components must synchronize to an accuracy of ≤100 ms (Requirement R8).
  The standard does not prescribe **uncertainty quantification methods**, **error detection/correction mechanisms**, or **quality assurance processes**, leaving these as implementation-specific considerations. The **entity-based architecture** facilitates modular testing but does not enforce standardized validation protocols."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined by the following technical specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **ROS2 Network**: Internal software component communication occurs within a **ROS2** network, enabling modular, real-time data exchange between functional elements (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Data Translation*).
   - **Wi-Fi + UDP**: Inter-entity communication (Physical Twin → Digital Twin and vice versa) uses **Wi-Fi** at the network layer with **UDP** at the transport layer (as partially described in the document).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical**: Data flows from Physical Twin sensors → Digital Twin software components → User Interface, with clear separation between internal (ROS2) and external (Wi-Fi/UDP) communication layers.
   - **Decentralized**: Components communicate peer-to-peer within ROS2, while Wi-Fi/UDP handles broader entity-level data exchange.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Latency**: The document mentions a **100 ms** latency requirement (R7) for real-time monitoring and control, though specific bandwidth values (e.g., Mbps) are not provided.
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for applications like ACC, where timely data processing and command execution are essential.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document. Reliability may be inherent in ROS2’s Quality of Service (QoS) mechanisms or Wi-Fi’s inherent redundancy, but specifics are absent.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not described in the provided documents. Security measures (e.g., encryption, access control) are not mentioned for either ROS2 or Wi-Fi/UDP communication.

**Key Technologies**:
- **ROS2**: For internal software communication.
- **Wi-Fi + UDP**: For inter-entity data transmission.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: The Physical Twin Entity (e.g., vehicle sensors, actuators) hosts lightweight components (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Actuation FE*) locally to minimize latency.
   - **Digital Twin Entity**: Likely deployed on a **workstation or server** external to the vehicle (as implied by the need for 3D visualization and user interfaces). Specific platforms (e.g., cloud, on-premise) are not detailed.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Physical Twin (Vehicle)**:
     - **Hardware**: Includes sensors (LiDAR, cameras) and embedded systems (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson for preprocessing).
     - **Computational**: Real-time processing of point clouds and vehicle state data.
   - **Digital Twin (Workstation/Server)**:
     - **Storage**: Data is stored in an **SQL database** (as inferred from Table I’s *Data Storage* entry under *Reporting FE*).
     - **Computational**: Supports visualization (e.g., 3D rendering of vehicle and environment), data translation, and user interface operations.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly described. Scalability may be achieved through modular ROS2 components or distributed storage, but specifics are absent.

4. **Deployment Models**:
   - **Hybrid**: Combines edge (vehicle) and centralized (workstation/server) deployment. The *Data Translation* component acts as a bridge between these layers.
   - **External Storage**: Data is stored external to the vehicle (e.g., for reporting, analysis, or long-term retention).

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - Not detailed in the document. Reliability may depend on redundant sensor inputs, ROS2’s fault-tolerant mechanisms, or Wi-Fi connectivity, but no explicit assurances are provided.

**Key Components**:
- **NVIDIA Jetson**: Used on the physical side for preprocessing.
- **SQL Database**: For structured data storage on the Digital Twin side.
- **ROS2 Network**: Enables modular and scalable deployment of software components.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard (2021)**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - An **entity-based architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection/Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**.
  - **Interoperability standards** are implied via modular **Functional Entities (FEs)**, though no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MTConnect) are mandated—only **OPC Unified Architecture** is mentioned as a *possible* implementation choice.
  - **Compliance frameworks** are not detailed beyond ISO 23247, and **certification requirements** are not referenced. The standard emphasizes **information exchange** (Part 4) but does not specify **validation or certification bodies**. **Industry-specific standards** (e.g., automotive, aerospace) are not addressed in the provided context."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, specifying performance requirements and test procedures to ensure safe operation. Key safety considerations include **network latency constraints**, where the **Digital Twin System (DTS)** must maintain a **maximum latency of 100 ms** (as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15**). The system must also comply with **functional safety standards** as referenced in **ISO 26262-1:2018**, though explicit implementation details (e.g., ASIL levels, fail-safe mechanisms) are not provided in the documents. No explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols, access control, authentication, or threat mitigation** is found in the provided references. The **Dalibor et al. (2020)** paper discusses **model-driven architecture for interactive digital twin cockpits** but does not specify **HIL/SIL testing methodologies** or **safety-critical system considerations** beyond the broader context of digital twin integration."
]
